title: potentilla alpestris var. sabauda
intro: Potentilla alpestris var. sabauda, also known as the Alpine Cinquefoil, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and roots have been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, and are believed to have health benefits including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Potentilla alpestris var. sabauda include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
